Richard Trott, Sr.
(28 August 1584 – 11 October 1669)
memoryArtifacts.description:
Richard Treat, Esq., the immigrant ancestor of the Treat Family of Wethersfield, Connecticut, the youngest known child of Robert & Honora Treat of Pitminster, Somersetshire, England. 

He was bapt. Au …

nameMeanings.name: Lambert
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English, French, Walloon, Dutch, German, Polish, Czech, and Slovak: from a personal name composed of the ancient Germanic elements land ‘land, territory’ + berht ‘bright, famous’. In England, the native Old English form Landbeorht was replaced by Lambert, the Continental form of the name that was taken to England by the Normans from France. The name gained wider currency in Britain in the Middle Ages with the immigration of weavers from Flanders, among whom Saint Lambert or Lamprecht, bishop of Maastricht in around 700, was a popular cult figure. In Italy the name was popularized in the Middle Ages as a result of the fame of Lambert I and II, Dukes of Spoleto and Holy Roman Emperors. Lambert is the second most frequent surname in Wallonia. In North America, the English form of the surname has absorbed cognates from other languages, for example Slovenian Lampret and Lampreht (see also Lampert ).

History: Aubin Lambert dit Champagne from Tourouvre in Orne, France, married Élisabeth Aubert in Quebec City, QC, in 1670; see also Champagne . Eustache Lambert from Boulogne-sur-Mer in Pas-de-Calais, France, married Marie Laurence in France c. 1656, died in Quebec City, QC, in 1673. Pierre Lambert from Fourmetot in Eure, France, married Marie Normand in Quebec City, QC, in 1680. Augustin Hébert dit Lambert, a descendant of Augustin Hébert from Paris, France, married Françoise Petit in Varennes, QC, in 1761. — This surname is listed in the (US) National Huguenot Society's register of qualified Huguenot ancestors and also in the similar register of the Huguenot Society of America.

variants.name: Lambers
meanings: Dutch: patronymic from the personal name   Lambert  .

variants.name: Lamberth
meanings: English: variant of   Lambert  .

variants.name: Lambertus
meanings: German and Dutch: humanistic name, Latinized form of the personal name   Lambert  .

variants.name: Lamberti
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Antonio, Carlo, Gaetano, Luciano, Amedeo, Angelo, Biagio, Domenic, Donato, Enrico, Gino, Iolanda.
Italian: patronymic or plural form of the personal name Lamberto, an equivalent of Lambert . This surname is also found in Corsica and southern France.
German and Dutch: Latinized (humanistic) patronymic from the personal name   Lambert  , using the genitive of its Latinized form   Lambertus  .

variants.name: Lamberty
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Ana, Dimas, Jose, Juan, Altagracia, Angel, Blanca, Efrain, Fernando, Jaime, Ramon.
German and French: Latinized (humanistic) patronymic from the personal name Lambert , from Lamberti, the genitive of its Latinized form Lambertus . Compare Lamberti 2.

variants.name: Lambertz
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Heinz, Erna, Johann, Ralf.
North German: patronymic from   Lambert  .

variants.name: Lamberto
meanings: Italian and Spanish: from the personal name Lamberto (see Lambert ).

variants.name: Lampert
meanings: English, North German, Dutch, and Slovak; Hungarian (Lampért): variant of Lambert . In North America, the English form of the surname has absorbed some cognates from other languages, for example Slovenian Lampret and Lampreht.
English: variant of Lambard, an occupational name from Middle English lambherd, lambhird, lambhurd ‘lamb-herd’ (Old English lamb + hierde).

variants.name: Lambertsen
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Scandinavian Erik.
Dutch, Danish, North German, and Norwegian: patronymic from the personal name   Lambert  . This surname has died out in the Netherlands.
History: Cornelius Lambertsen came from Utrecht, the Netherlands, arriving at New Amsterdam in New Netherland (now New York City, NY) in 1642.

variants.name: Lamb
meanings: English: from the Middle English personal name Lamb, a pet form of Lambert .
English: nickname for a meek and inoffensive person, from Middle English lamb, or a metonymic occupational name for a keeper of lambs. See also Lamm .

Irish: shortened Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó Luain ‘descendant of the warrior’, formerly Anglicized as O'Loan (see Lane 3). MacLysaght comments: "The form Lamb(e), which results from a more than usually absurd pseudo-translation (uan ‘lamb’), is now much more numerous than O'Loan itself.".

nameMeanings.name: Honour
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From the vocabulary word denoting the quality (via Old French, from Latin honor). The name was popular with the Puritans in the 17th century and has remained in modest use to the present day.

variants.name: Honor
meanings: Variant spelling of   Honour ;  the dominant spelling of both the vocabulary word and the given name in the United States today. This is now the more frequent spelling of the name in Britain also, used, for example, for the actress  Honor Blackman  (  b. 1926 ).

historicEvents.title: Book of Common Prayer
historicEvents.description: Book of Common Prayer, a product of the English Reformation, was published in 1549 for assistance in the administration of the sacraments and other rites and ceremonies of the church according to the use of the Church of England. The book outlined morning, evening, and communion prayers and orders for baptism and marriage, thus making England a truly Protestant state.

historicEvents.title: Act of Uniformity
historicEvents.description: The Act of Uniformity was passed by the Parliament of England and required all people to go to church once a week. The consequence of not attending church was a fine of 12 pence, which was a considerable amount for a poor person.

historicEvents.title: State Lottery
historicEvents.description: A State Lottery was recorded in 1569. The tickets were sold at St. Paul's Cathedral in London.

historicEvents.title: The Royal Exchange is Organized
historicEvents.description: The Royal Exchange was organized in January 1571 by Sir Thomas Gresham. Gresham is known as the father of English banking.

historicEvents.title: The Honourable East India Company
historicEvents.description: The Honourable East India Company, a British joint-stock company, was established in 1600 for trade in the Indian Ocean region. At its height, the British East India Company had a private army which was twice the size of the British Army, ruled large sections of India, and revenues in the millions.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Historic UK - The East India Company

historicEvents.title: The Pilgrims Board the Mayflower
historicEvents.description: "The Pilgrims boarded the Mayflower in search of religious freedom in the New World in September 1620. The Mayflower set sail from Plymouth, England, with 102 passengers, 40 of whom called themselves ""Saints."" After two stormy months at sea, the ship reached the New World. Nearly half of the group died during their first winter due to malnutrition and the harsh New England winter."

en.lifeSummary: Honour Lambert married Robert Treat in 1563, in Pitminster, Somerset, England. They were the parents of at least 2 sons and 5 daughters. She died on 17 September 1627, in Pitminster, Somerset, England, and was buried in Pitminster, Somerset, England, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Honour Lambert a épousé Robert Treat en 1563, à Pitminster, Somerset, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils et 5 filles. Elle est décédée le 17 septembre 1627, à Pitminster, Somerset, Angleterre, et a été enterrée à Pitminster, Somerset, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

de.lifeSummary: Honour Lambert hat 1563 in Pitminster, Somerset, England Robert Treat geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 2 Söhne und 5 Töchter. Sie starb am 17. September 1627 in Pitminster, Somerset, England und wurde in Pitminster, Somerset, England, United Kingdom begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Honour Lambert lei sposato Robert Treat nel 1563, nel Pitminster, Somerset, England. Erano genitori di almeno 2 figli e 5 figlie. Morì il 17 settembre 1627, nel Pitminster, Somerset, England, e fu sepolta a Pitminster, Somerset, Inghilterra, Regno Unito.

ja.lifeSummary: Honour Lambertは1563年にイギリスソメルセトピトミンスターでRobert Treatと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも2人の息子と5人の娘の親であった。彼女は1627年9月17日にイギリスソメルセトピトミンスターで死去し，イギリスイギリスソメルセトピトミンスターにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Honour Lambert(은)는 1563년 영국 서머세트 피트민스터에서 Robert Treat(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들과 다섯 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1627년 9월 17일 영국 서머세트 피트민스터에서 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 서머세트 피트민스터에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Honour Lambert casou-se com Robert Treat em 1563, em Pitminster, Somerset, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 2 filhos e 5 filhas. Ela faleceu em 17 de setembro de 1627, em Pitminster, Somerset, Inglaterra, e foi sepultada em Pitminster, Somerset, Inglaterra, United Kingdom.

ru.lifeSummary: Honour Lambert вышла замуж за человека по имени Robert Treat в 1563 г. в местности питминстир, сомирсет, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына и 5 дочерей. Она умерла 17 сентября 1627 г. в местности питминстир, сомирсет, Англия и была похоронена в местности питминстир, сомирсет, Англия, Великобритания.

es.lifeSummary: Honour Lambert se casó con Robert Treat en 1563, en Pitminster, Somerset, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 2 hijos y 5 hijas. Murió el 17 de septiembre de 1627, en Pitminster, Somerset, Inglaterra, y fue sepultada en Pitminster, Somerset, Inglaterra, Reino Unido.
